task_completion.description: The provided sales data is opened and visible in the spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc is open, displaying the sales data (January to June, with values ranging from 12500 to 18500).

task_completion.description: A chart object is created in the spreadsheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A chart object appears on the spreadsheet canvas, or the Chart Wizard dialog is actively being used.

task_completion.description: The created chart is a bar or column chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chart visually displays rectangular bars (either vertical or horizontal), or the Chart Wizard shows 'Bar' or 'Column' selected as the chart type.

task_completion.description: The chart correctly represents the provided sales data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chart displays exactly 6 bars corresponding to the 6 months of data, with the axis scaled appropriately (up to at least 18500).
An export or save dialog is visible saving the file as 'sales_chart.png' in the Documents folder, or a terminal/file manager confirms the file exists.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to create the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice Calc menus, toolbars, or the Chart Wizard, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script (e.g., Python/matplotlib) to bypass the spreadsheet software.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown running scripts to generate the chart image directly.

integrity.description: The chart is a genuine spreadsheet object, not a pasted image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart is seen being created via the Chart Wizard or has spreadsheet chart selection handles, rather than being an imported static image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Task requires creating a bar/column chart from 6 months of sales data (Jan: 12500, Feb: 15200, Mar: 14800, Apr: 16300, May: 17900, Jun: 18500) in LibreOffice Calc. Success criteria: 1) Chart exists, 2) Chart type is bar or column, 3) Chart data range points to the 6 rows of sales data, 4) Original sales data is preserved. The task description specifies saving as ~/Documents/sales_chart.png. Note: The automated verifier also checks for an ODS file, so saving the spreadsheet itself is also acceptable progress.
